title: The Cavalry of Rome
designIntent: DSK-composed WebGPU-first visual route: scan over a 3D Roman terrain map, hover large highlighted regions, click to trigger a cinematic dive, and reveal a low-poly battlefield tableau.
protectedExpressionGuardrails: Do not copy tabletop scenario layouts, card systems, dice faces, command sections, rule text, or unit-stat tables.
Keep the route visual-first until the cinematic terrain and battlefield proof is stable.
Use Roman-history flavor as original visual composition, not as a digital recreation of a board-game ruleset.

futureProtoKitCandidatesFromCustomLogic.source: procedural terrain mesh and terrain color logic
futureProtoKitCandidatesFromCustomLogic.reason: Reusable visual terrain foundation for campaign-map routes.

futureProtoKitCandidatesFromCustomLogic.source: large region overlay generation and hover highlighting
futureProtoKitCandidatesFromCustomLogic.reason: Reusable replacement for point-node strategy maps.

futureProtoKitCandidatesFromCustomLogic.source: world-map scan camera and cinematic dive interpolation
instanced low-poly soldier and banner layout generation
futureProtoKitCandidatesFromCustomLogic.reason: Reusable army-tableau visualization without combat simulation.

futureProtoKitCandidatesFromCustomLogic.source: WebGPU presentation adapter, terrain buffers, depth setup, and fallback seam
futureProtoKitCandidatesFromCustomLogic.reason: Reusable renderer adapter if more routes adopt WebGPU-first visuals.

futureProtoKitCandidatesFromCustomLogic.source: field fog, formation mood, banners, and pre-battle atmosphere descriptors
futureProtoKitCandidatesFromCustomLogic.reason: Reusable ambience layer for non-combat battlefield reveals.

nextLedge: Add a browser-backed route smoke for the live endpoint and then extract terrain-region highlight descriptors into a DOM-free proof seam.
